target.title: fix #77426: [Bug]:sessions_yield: always returns "No session context" on MCP/claude-cli agent runtime path (gateway tool resolver missing sessionId + onYield)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`sessions_yield` on the bundled MCP / CLI loopback path was constructed without the active run `sessionId` and `onYield` lifecycle callback, so the tool could return `No session context` instead of yielding the active CLI-backed run. After ClawSweeper review, the CLI result metadata also needed to stay consistent with lifecycle terminal events so subagent/session consumers do not complete or clean up a yielded run.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #77426 reports that MCP/claude-cli agent runtime paths cannot use `sessions_yield` correctly, which breaks the pause/resume path expected after subagent work. The lifecycle propagation fix closes the remaining author-side gap where a yielded CLI run could be marked paused in result meta but completed by lifecycle listeners.

What is the intended outcome?

A loopback MCP `tools/call` for `sessions_yield` with the run session header returns `{ status: "yielded" }`, records yield state for the active CLI run, exposes paused/end-turn result meta, and emits matching lifecycle `end` metadata so downstream wait/lifecycle code keeps the run paused.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change embedded-runner yield behavior, public user config, migrations, provider routing, or tool visibility policy.

What does success look like?

CLI bundle MCP config carries `x-openclaw-session-id`; MCP request context and gateway scoped tool resolution preserve that session id and yield callback; loopback tool cache entries refresh for each registered yield context; CLI results and lifecycle terminal events are marked `yielded: true`, `livenessState: "paused"`, and `stopReason: "end_turn"` after loopback yield.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the process-local yield context ownership in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts`, the `sessionId` / yield-context cache key in `src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts`, the CLI yielded/end-turn meta mapping in `src/agents/cli-runner.ts`, and the lifecycle propagation in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #77426

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #77426

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Picked from the public bug issue queue; the latest lifecycle propagation and registry coverage updates address ClawSweeper author-action findings on this PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_yield` on the MCP/CLI loopback path no longer returns `No session context`; it records yield state for the active run and now keeps lifecycle consumers aligned with the yielded/paused state.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto current `origin/main` after resolving merge conflicts, at PR head `7a1e8c0aea`, using the real MCP loopback HTTP server and gateway tool resolver.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<start MCP loopback server, POST tools/call sessions_yield with x-openclaw-session-id, assert yielded context>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Fresh terminal capture from the rebased-head MCP loopback smoke run.

  ```text
  Validation `rebased-http-loopback-sessions-yield-smoke` passed.
  HTTP POST /mcp tools/call sessions_yield returned:
  {"httpStatus":200,"rpcIsError":false,"toolResult":{"status":"yielded","message":"rebased http smoke yield"},"yielded":true,"message":"rebased http smoke yield"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The HTTP loopback MCP tool call returned a non-error JSON-RPC result with `status: "yielded"`, and the run yield context recorded `yielded: true` with the same message. The lifecycle fix keeps yielded state present on CLI lifecycle terminal events.
- What was not tested: A full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call itself. The proof calls the same local HTTP MCP endpoint and gateway tool resolver directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The local config emitted unrelated stale plugin warnings during the smoke run; the loopback MCP request still completed with HTTP 200 and the expected yielded state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Added RED tests first. Before the production changes, targeted tests showed missing `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, missing `sessionId` in MCP request/scoped resolver context, no `onYield` callback invocation from gateway-scoped `sessions_yield`, and lifecycle terminal events missing yielded/paused metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx --eval '<HTTP loopback sessions_yield smoke>'`
  - Fresh rebased-head result at `7a1e8c0aea`: passed with `httpStatus: 200`, `toolResult.status: "yielded"`, `yielded: true`.
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`
  - Fresh rebased-head result at `7a1e8c0aea`: gateway/auto-reply/agents shards passed; 12 test files passed and 355 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - Earlier post-sync result before this conflict-only rebase: passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.request.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`
  - Earlier post-sync result before this conflict-only rebase: passed. A broad local `pnpm lint --threads=8` rerun was attempted but did not complete before the local oxlint core shard timed out, so it is not counted as passing proof.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: CLI bundle MCP env/config now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` and passes `sessionId` into prompt-time loopback scoped tool resolution.
- `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`: MCP HTTP headers now propagate `x-openclaw-session-id`, loopback tool cache entries are separated by session id, and consecutive yielded runs with the same session id refresh cached yield tools inside the cache TTL.
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`: gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` receives `sessionId` + `onYield` and returns `status: "yielded"`.
- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: CLI run result meta is marked yielded/paused/end_turn when bundle MCP records a yield.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts`: CLI lifecycle `end` events now carry yielded/paused/end_turn metadata from the final CLI result.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`: subagent registry keeps a CLI lifecycle-yielded run paused instead of completing, announcing no output, or dispatching cleanup.

What failed before this fix, if known?

RED tests failed because the CLI env lacked `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, gateway request context passed `sessionId: undefined`, gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` never invoked `onYield`, and CLI lifecycle terminal events did not include yielded/paused/end_turn metadata. After initial PR submission, CI `check-lint` also failed on two lint-only issues introduced by this PR; both were reproduced locally and fixed.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the CLI prepare path, HTTP loopback path, gateway resolver behavior, CLI run result meta, CLI lifecycle event propagation, and subagent registry lifecycle consumption.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. CLI-backed MCP `sessions_yield` can now pause a run instead of reporting missing session context, and lifecycle consumers keep that yielded run paused instead of completing it.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Internal bundled MCP config/env now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` / `x-openclaw-session-id`. No user config or migration behavior changes.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The local MCP loopback tool execution path now carries run session/yield context. The existing bearer auth and loopback-only HTTP checks remain unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Process-local yield state and cached loopback tool instances could leak across runs, or yielded result state could diverge from lifecycle terminal events if one path is updated without the other.

How is that risk mitigated?

Yield handlers are resolved by run `sessionId`; loopback tool cache keys include `sessionId` and the registered yield-context cache key; the CLI runner clears the exact registered yield context in `finally`; and CLI lifecycle terminal events now reuse the final result meta's yielded/paused/end_turn state.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after conflict resolution, PR body refresh, and fresh rebased-head real-behavior proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Fresh real-behavior proof and targeted Vitest coverage were rerun on the rebased conflict-resolution head. Remote CI/ClawSweeper should be used for the pushed branch head; the only remaining proof limitation is the disclosed absence of a full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001: The `status: waiting on author` signal is addressed by this author update; it should be reassessed after PR refresh.
- RF-002: Added regression coverage proving subagent registry does not complete a CLI-yielded lifecycle run.
- RF-003/RF-005: Propagated CLI yield state into lifecycle terminal events so lifecycle consumers receive the same yielded/paused/end_turn state as result metadata.
- RF-004: Kept the repair narrow: lifecycle propagation plus focused tests, with no product/API decision required.
- RF-006: Ran the requested `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts` validation successfully.
- RF-007: Ran `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src` successfully.

target.title: fix(plugins): guard runtime boundary manifest rows
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden runtime plugin boundary lookup so malformed manifest rows are skipped per row instead of aborting healthy runtime resolution.
- Add focused regression coverage for poisoned manifest accessors in both plugin-id lookup and runtime entry-base-name lookup.

## Verification

- Red repro before fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.test.ts --reporter=verbose` failed with `runtime boundary manifest id exploded` and `runtime boundary manifest source exploded`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.test.ts src/plugins/runtime-plugin-boundary.whatsapp.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a stale or malformed plugin manifest row with an unreadable `id` or `source` accessor could throw during runtime boundary lookup and prevent a later healthy plugin runtime from resolving.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree using the repo Vitest wrapper and focused plugin runtime boundary fixtures.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-plugin-boundary.test.ts src/plugins/runtime-plugin-boundary.whatsapp.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: the focused shard passed with 2 files and 5 tests after the fix, and branch autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: runtime boundary lookup skips poisoned manifest rows and still resolves the healthy plugin runtime record by plugin id and by `light-runtime-api` / `runtime-api` entry names.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` could not run remotely because Blacksmith is not authenticated in this shell and Crabbox coordinator requests returned HTTP 401 before lease creation.

target.title: feat(usage): native templated /usage full footer renderer
target.body:
> **Stacks on #89629** (the `usageState` per-turn contract). The first commit here is #89629; review the final commit (`feat(usage): native templated /usage full renderer`). Merge after #89629.

## What
Render the per-reply `/usage full` footer **in core** from a declarative template, instead of requiring a renderer plugin. When `messages.usageTemplate` is set, the footer is produced from it; otherwise the existing built-in line is used unchanged. Fail-open: an absent / unreadable / invalid template, or an empty render, falls back to the built-in line.

```jsonc
"messages": {
  // path (supports ~) …
  "usageTemplate": "~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json"
  // … or an inline template object
}
```

The template format (`openclaw.usageBar.v1`) is a small declarative DSL: named glyph `scales`, `aliases` (lookup-with-echo), and per-channel `output.surfaces` piece-lists, with verbs `num` / `dur` / `pct` / `inv` / `alias` / `meter` (incl. `meter:1` single glyph and per-window `item_scales`). It is a translator over the contract — no embedded executable; the only external surface is template *data*.

## How
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/translator.ts` — the engine. Pure, fail-open, **codepoint-correct** glyph indexing (JS string indexing would split astral emoji).
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/contract.ts` — `buildUsageContract`: the per-turn `usageState` snapshot → the `openclaw.usageLine.v1` contract the template reads.
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/template.ts` — resolve from a path (mtime-cached) or an inline object.
- `agent-runner.ts` — capture the per-turn snapshot once; at the `/usage full` branch, render the template when configured, else keep the built-in line.
- `messages.usageTemplate` config field + strict zod schema.

No behaviour change unless `messages.usageTemplate` is set.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Customizing the footer today requires a renderer **plugin** that core spawns as a subprocess per reply. This PR renders the footer **in core** from a declarative template (data, not an executable). Opt-in via `messages.usageTemplate`; unset = built-in line unchanged; any error fails open to the built-in line.

**Real environment tested**: MarvinMBP, production OpenClaw `2026.6.1-beta.2` install at `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist`. The `usage-footer` **plugin is disabled** in the live config and `messages.usageTemplate` points at the live `~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json`, so the footer the gateway emits is the in-core path under test.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the renderer (`translator` / `contract` / `template`) and rendered the live template against a representative per-turn snapshot for both surfaces, then exercised the fail-open paths — no plugin, no subprocess:
```console
$ node --import tsx render-proof.mts     # loadUsageBarTemplate("~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json") -> buildUsageContract(snapshot, surface) -> renderUsageBar(...)
$ grep -rl 'function renderUsageBar' /usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist
$ jq '.plugins.entries["usage-footer"].enabled, .messages.usageTemplate' ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
```

**Evidence after fix**: console output against the live install + live template:
```console
template loaded from live path: true

[discord] core-rendered footer:
-# -
-# 🩺 gpt5.5✏️ | med🐌 | 📚 [⣿⣿⣿⣿⣷]272k | ↕ 10k/21🗄 94%📊 🌤4h45m 🍀6.6d

[telegram] core-rendered footer:
—
gpt5.5 ✏️ med 🐌 |[⣿⣿⣿⣿⣷]272k
📊 🌤4h45m 🍀6.6d

# fail-open (renderUsageBar never throws; "" -> core's built-in line):
null template                -> ""
non-object template          -> ""
throwing getter in pieces    -> ""
empty object                 -> ""
loadUsageBarTemplate({})     -> undefined   (custom line skipped entirely)
```
Live install confirms this is the in-core path, not the plugin: `grep -rl 'function renderUsageBar' .../dist` -> `dist/agent-runner.runtime-Djok-Gt3.js`; `usage-footer` plugin `enabled = false`; `messages.usageTemplate = ~/.openclaw/.usage_bar.json`.

**Observed result after fix**: Core turns the user-supplied template **data** into the exact multi-line custom footer for both Discord and Telegram (aliases `gpt-5.5`->`gpt5.5`/`medium`->`med`, override `✏️`, braille context meter, per-window `weather`/`plants` limit glyphs) with the renderer plugin disabled. Malformed, throwing, or empty templates render `""` and core falls back to its built-in line; an unset `usageTemplate` skips the custom path. A footer **screenshot is intentionally not the proof**: it cannot show *which* code rendered the line — the disabled plugin + the shipped `renderUsageBar` reproducing the string byte-for-byte can.

**What was not tested**: Live per-surface delivery fan-out beyond Discord/Telegram. Known sharp edge: an *unknown verb* degrades to literal text (e.g. `bogusverb:::`) rather than dropping the segment — graceful and non-throwing, but not silent; tightening that is a follow-up. Unit tests (`usage-bar/translator.test.ts`: verb parity, all segment forms, astral-glyph correctness) and `run-tsgo` clean / schema `--check` ok are supplemental, not the proof.

## Why
Lets an operator fully customize the usage footer (per channel, multi-line, glyphs, aliases) with config + a data template, with zero runtime cost when unused and no plugin/subprocess. Pairs with #89629 (the data) and the optional persistent-`/usage` default (#89762).

target.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

---

*AI Disclosure: This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude). All code was reviewed and tested by a human.*

target.title: feat(feishu): comprehensive plugin enhancements — streaming, dedup, skills, calendar, and stability
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates all Feishu plugin enhancements developed across multiple iterations (supersedes #38770, #38772, #39409, #39440). All previous PRs were closed without merging; this PR contains the complete changeset.

---

## Issues Fixed

### Streaming Card Display

1. **Text Duplication in Streaming Cards**: `mergeStreamingText()` fallback path duplicated cumulative text → bypass it, treat `onPartialReply` text as complete state
2. **Content Duplication Across Cards**: each card showed cumulative text instead of its own block → track `blockOffset`, slice cumulative text per card
3. **Content Disappearing on Card Close**: `closeStreaming()` used per-block text while card displayed cumulative text → `streamText` consistently uses sliced card text
4. **Missing Tail Text After Last Block**: `deliver(kind=final)` sent full duplicate or was skipped → extract only undelivered tail `text.slice(blockOffset)`, send if non-empty
5. **Extra Streaming Card After Last Block**: `onPartialReply` kept creating new cards → only start new card if substantial new content beyond `blockOffset`
6. **Slow Streaming Updates**: `await this.queue` blocked `onPartialReply` on API round-trips; no flush timer → remove `await` (fire-and-forget), add `pendingFlushTimer` (300ms auto-flush)
7. **Markdown Crash Guard**: `isSafeToRender()` blocks updates during unclosed code fences
8. **Typing Indicator Emoji**: Changed from `Typing` to `Get` (fix reaction 231001 error)
9. **Broken CardKit Streaming**: Replaced broken CardKit chunked patching with Semantic Block Delivery using `block` events from `reply-dispatcher.ts`

### Message Deduplication (3-Layer)

10. **event_id Dedup**: Lark SDK `EventDispatcher.parse()` flattens header into callback data — `event_id` IS available. Replaced content-hash workaround with proper `event_id` dedup as recommended by Feishu docs
11. **Stale Message Filtering in Debounce**: When debounce batch has messages with >2min time gap, drop older messages and record their IDs in persistent dedup
12. **create_time-based Stale Rejection**: Feishu retries re-deliver messages with new `message_id`/`event_id` but same `create_time`. Track last processed `create_time` per `chat+sender` and reject stale duplicates

### Message Control & Abort

13. **Fast Abort Bypass**: Trigger words (`stop`, `停止`, `取消`, `esc`, etc.) skip 2s debounce + bypass `chatQueue` → direct dispatch to `tryFastAbortFromMessage`
14. **Message Recall Abort**: Register `im.message.recalled_v1` → clear debounce buffer, record in dedup, synthesize `/stop` command via `dispatchFeishuMessageDirect`

### Feishu Ops Tools (Skills)

15. **feishu_contacts**: Search contacts with auto-sync from API
16. **feishu_groups**: List/search bot groups with auto-sync
17. **feishu_send**: Send text/file/@all to users or groups
18. **feishu_calendar**: Create, **update**, and **delete** calendar events by `event_id`
19. **2-Tier Contact Sync**: When list API returns 0 results, fallback to BFS department traversal + per-department user fetch (ported from `feishu-ops/scripts/update-contacts.py`)
20. **SKILL.md Rewrite**: All skill docs converted to English with JSON tool-call examples

### Code Quality

21. **Error Messages Standardized to English** across all new modules
22. **Group Search Hint**: `searchGroupsOrSync()` lists all bot groups and prompts user to add bot to target group if not found
23. **Mention Logic**: More resilient verify mention logic and fix log propagation
24. **SDK v1.30+ Response Parsing**: Fix `proactive-send.ts` to handle Lark SDK top-level response format (`{ file_key }` instead of `{ code: 0, data: { file_key } }`)

### Other

25. **Daemon Working Directory**: Resolve gateway working directory to installation root

---

## Files Changed

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` | event_id dedup, create_time dedup, fast abort bypass, recall handler, mention logic |
| `extensions/feishu/src/dedup.ts` | `checkAndRecordCreateTime()`, create_time tracking per chat+sender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts` | Block-boundary card splitting, blockOffset tracking, tail text, semantic block delivery |
| `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts` | Remove mergeStreamingText, async queue, pendingFlushTimer, isSafeToRender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/typing.ts` | Change typing emoji to Get |
| `extensions/feishu/src/inbound-debounce.ts` | Stale message filtering, removeFromBuffer() |
| `extensions/feishu/src/feishu-ops-tools.ts` | Register contacts, groups, send, calendar tools |
| `extensions/feishu/src/contacts.ts` | 2-tier sync with department fallback |
| `extensions/feishu/src/calendar.ts` | Update and delete calendar events |
| `extensions/feishu/src/proactive-send.ts` | SDK v1.30+ response parsing fix |
| `extensions/feishu/skills/*/SKILL.md` | English rewrite with JSON examples |
| `src/daemon/program-args.ts` | Working directory resolution fix |

## Supersedes

- #38770 (CLOSED — streaming card fix)
- #38772 (CLOSED — streaming + event_id dedup)
- #39409 (CLOSED — message control, skills, stability)
- #39440 (CLOSED — calendar update/delete)

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: fix(memory): attribute corpus=all timeouts to the slow branch instead of the provider
target.body:
## Summary

- `memory_search` with `corpus=all` could hit the 15s tool deadline and return a "Memory search is unavailable due to an embedding/provider error" warning even when the embedding provider probe and every individual corpus searched successfully.
- The warning now reflects that the tool timed out and names the slow branch (`memory/session` vs `wiki/supplement`), so users stop chasing a healthy provider config.

## Motivation

Closes #92633.

The tool-level deadline wrapper (`runMemorySearchToolWithDeadline`) returns `error: "memory_search timed out after 15s"`, which is funneled through `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult`. That helper unconditionally attributed any non-quota failure to an embedding/provider error and told users to "Check embedding provider configuration". For a `corpus=all` aggregation stall this is misleading: the provider probe, `corpus=memory`, `corpus=sessions`, and `corpus=wiki` all succeed individually — only the combined path times out.

The search tool already tracks which corpus branch was active when a failure occurred (`activeUnavailablePhase` / `failedUnavailablePhase`). This change:

- Detects the deadline timeout message in `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult` and emits a timeout-specific warning + action instead of the provider-error text (issue ask #3).
- Threads the failed phase through to name the slow branch and exposes `debug.timedOut` / `debug.phase` (issue ask #4).
- Leaves quota errors and genuine provider errors on their existing messages.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` — 34 passed (covers manager-setup timeout, memory-branch timeout + cooldown, abort-aware timeout, and the two existing `corpus=all` stall-in-wiki / stall-in-memory cases now asserting accurate attribution).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts` — 877 + 129 passed (no regressions; active-memory provider-error path unchanged).
- `oxlint` + `oxfmt --check` clean on all 5 changed files.
- tsgo extensions test project type-check: clean.

## Did NOT change

- Quota (`insufficient_quota`/`429`) and non-timeout provider-error messages are byte-identical to before.
- The 15s deadline value and abort behavior are untouched — this is attribution only, not a timeout-tuning change.

target.title: fix(cron): allow timeoutSeconds: 0 for no-timeout mode
target.body:
## 🐛 Problem

The cron job UI validation logic incorrectly rejects `timeoutSeconds: 0`, which should represent "no timeout" mode.

**Current behavior:**
```typescript
if (timeout <= 0) {
  errors.timeoutSeconds = "cron.errors.timeoutInvalid";
}
```
This prevents users from setting `timeoutSeconds = 0` to disable timeout entirely.

## ✅ Solution

Update validation to allow `0` as a valid value (meaning "no timeout"):

```typescript
// 0 = no timeout, > 0 = timeout in seconds
if (timeout !== 0 && timeout <= 0) {
  errors.timeoutSeconds = "cron.errors.timeoutInvalid";
}
```

## 📁 Files Changed

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts` - Updated `validateCronForm` function

## 🧪 Testing

- Create a cron job with `timeoutSeconds: 0`
- Verify it saves without validation errors
- Confirm the job runs without timeout restrictions

## 📊 Impact

- **User-facing**: Users can now disable timeout for long-running cron jobs
- **Backward compatible**: Existing jobs with `timeoutSeconds > 0` are unaffected
- **Risk**: Low - single line change, clear semantic intent

## 📝 Related

Closes #41272

target.title: feat: implement shadow-logger plugin for memory system
target.body:
## Description
This PR implements the `shadow-logger` plugin, which provides the Layer 1 (Episodic Memory) of the new multi-layered memory system for OpenClaw. 

The plugin "taps" into the OpenClaw internal hook system to capture all incoming (`message:received`) and outgoing (`message:sent`) messages. These messages are then logged to a Supabase database, enabling asynchronous distillation into semantic memory layers later.

## Technical Details
- **Implementation:** A new OpenClaw plugin in `extensions/shadow-logger/`.
- **Integration:** Uses `registerInternalHook` to listen to message events.
- **Storage:** Uses Supabase for persistent storage of raw dialogues.
- **Schema:**
    - `dialogues` table: Stores raw message payloads, including conversation context and metadata.

## Documentation
Detailed technical specification can be found here: [OpenClaw Memory System - Technical Implementation Specification](https://app.notion.com/p/OpenClaw-Memory-System-Technical-Implementation-Specification-37df97eb93ff81538079da4bfb131619)

## Checklist
- [x] Implement `shadow-logger.plugin.ts`
- [x] Configure plugin manifest and package
- [x] Ensure plugin is discoverable

target.title: fix(dreaming): increase narrative timeout from 60s to 120s for ARM devices (fixes #92494)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?

Increases `NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS` in the dreaming narrative generator from 60s to 120s. ARM devices with 600+ skills need ~57s just for cold-loading tool definitions on the first narrative phase, leaving almost no headroom within the 60s timeout for the actual LLM call.

## Related Issue

Fixes #92494

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made

- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`: Increased `NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS` from `60_000` to `120_000`, updated comment to document ARM cold-load scenario
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`: Updated timeout assertion from `60_000` to `120_000`

## How to Test

1. On an ARM device with 600+ skills, trigger a dreaming cron run
2. Observe that the light narrative phase completes within the new 120s timeout
3. Verify that stalled subagents are still cleaned up within 2 minutes

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
ARM devices with 600+ skills experience consistent narrative timeouts during dreaming light phase because cold-loading tool definitions takes ~57s, leaving only ~3s headroom within the 60s `NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS` limit for the actual LLM call. The timeout was previously increased from 15s to 60s but is still insufficient for resource-constrained environments.

**Real environment tested:**
macOS 26.4.1, Node v22, OpenClaw dev build from upstream/main (7190fc4d)

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```
pnpm build
node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const src = fs.readFileSync('extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts','utf8'); const m = src.match(/NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS\s*=\s*(\d[\d_]*\d)/); console.log('NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS =', m ? m[1] : 'NOT FOUND');"
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const src = fs.readFileSync('extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts','utf8'); const m = src.match(/NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS\s*=\s*(\d[\d_]*\d)/); console.log('NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS =', m ? m[1] : 'NOT FOUND');"
NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000

$ grep -n "NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS" extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts
104:const NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000;
957:            timeoutMs: NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS,
```

**Observed result after fix:**
Build succeeds. The narrative timeout constant is now 120_000ms (120s), doubling the previous 60s limit. The comment documents the ARM cold-load scenario. Test file assertion updated to match.

**What was not tested:**
- Live dreaming cron run on ARM device (requires ARM hardware with 600+ skills)
- Actual subagent timeout behavior with the new limit (requires full dreaming pipeline)

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Pin-from-here mirrors a session turn into pinned target threads by re-running each target channel's own dispatch with a bus-sourced reply resolver, so the mirror renders + persists natively (streaming/verbose per that channel's own config) instead of a flat post-hoc echo. - **Real environment tested**: A real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch in a Linux crabbox with a desktop; a real Telegram bot + a real Telegram account on Telegram Desktop; a real `claude-cli` backend (`sonnet-4-6`/`opus-4-8`, live Anthropic API) with exec tools; the tdlib user-driver sending real messages. This recreates the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow locally (same mechanism: telegram-desktop + user-driver + screen capture). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the gateway from the built branch; in a Telegram thread sent `/new`, `/verbose on`, `/pin on`; then drove the same session from webchat (`chat.send`) with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname`, and a two-step `date -u` + `uname -a` narrated before each command); screen-recorded Telegram Desktop and captured the gateway debug log. - **Evidence after fix**: Screenshots on fork branch `proof/pin-from-here-evidence` (linked below) plus gateway-log excerpts — `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn and the telegram mirror runs the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`), exactly once (post-hoc echo skipped by the handled-mark). - **Observed result after fix**: The pinned Telegram thread received the webchat-triggered turn's real reply — the actual `hostname` exec output (`crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`) — and, under the daily-driver verbose config, the full interleaved durable record (`💬` commentary + `🛠️` tool summaries) rendered natively, with exactly one delivery. - **What was not tested**: A current-head **live** Telegram capture of the later account-aware routing and destination-revocation/lifecycle changes — these are covered by unit tests but not yet filmed (multi-account needs a second test bot, and BotFather is currently rate-limiting bot creation). The screenshots below are from head `7454650`; the subsequent account/revocation/lifecycle fixes are unit-proven. Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). #### Riding the destination's verbose config Because the mirror re-runs each target's own dispatch, it inherits *that* account's streaming/verbose config rather than forcing anything. With the daily driver's setup (`verboseDefault: on`, `streaming.mode: progress`, reasoning + `toolProgressDetail: raw`), a webchat-triggered turn renders the **full interleaved durable record** in the pinned thread — assistant commentary (`💬`) and tool summaries (`🛠️`) as standalone messages, exactly as a native turn there would (matching #91976): This exercised a real gap: the CLI runner emits `stream:"tool"` events with both `phase:"start"` and `phase:"result"`, and the resolver was routing every tool event to `onToolStart` — so the durable `🛠️` summaries (driven by `onToolResult`) were dropped from the mirror while `💬` commentary still rendered. Fixed by running the bus tool events through the same `createCliToolSummaryTracker` the native CLI dispatch uses, so the mirror's tool summaries are byte-identical, in both streaming modes; the target dispatch still gates the actual send on verbose. ### Tests + CI - `tsgo:core`: clean. Full `oxlint` shard gate (`run-oxlint-shards`): clean across the PR surface (type-aware rules included). - The PR-relevant vitest matrix is green (`infra`, `auto-reply-*`, `gateway-core`/ `methods`, `commands`, `extension-telegram`, `hooks`, `plugin-sdk`): the bus-sourced resolver (incl. the tool-summary replay), handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. The `gateway-server` shard's auth/device-identity failures are pre-existing and environmental — identical `15 failed | 92 passed` on pristine `main` (merge-base `9827490`) in the same container. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so Telegram/forum reply facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata.

This is the Morty patch shape, polished for upstream:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty
- keeps replied-to body text out of the trusted metadata block and verbose trace
- folds in the hidden runtime-context routing from #89428 so current inbound/reply metadata no longer gets prepended into `promptForSession` / `promptForModel`

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Builds on the same routing direction as #89428. This branch includes the equivalent hidden-runtime-context behavior so the reply-specific contract can be reviewed as one coherent patch.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can carry `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the agent runtime needs a structured current-turn reply contract that stays available to the model without being persisted as user-authored prompt text.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`. The proof uses redacted Telegram/forum-style ids.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** First, built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the current-turn runtime prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

Then, exercised the hidden runtime-context routing with the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper and verified that reply metadata stays out of the submitted/persisted prompt strings while remaining in `runtimeContext`.

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF'
import { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts';
const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({
  effectivePrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  transcriptPrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  currentInboundContext: {
    text: 'Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=redacted-34971',
    reply: {
      currentMessageId: 'redacted-34974',
      replyToId: 'redacted-34971',
      replyTargetPresent: true,
      quotePresent: false,
      replyChainPresent: true,
      replyChainMessageIds: ['redacted-34971'],
    },
  },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  prompt: parts.prompt,
  modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt,
  promptContainsReplyMetadata: parts.prompt.includes('Current reply metadata') || parts.prompt.includes('redacted-34971'),
  modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata: (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('Current reply metadata') || (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('redacted-34971'),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('Current reply metadata')),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyToId: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('redacted-34971')),
}, null, 2));
PROOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the first real module command:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": ["34971"]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

Console output from the hidden runtime-context proof:

```json
{
  "prompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "modelPrompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, omitted from the visible/model prompt strings, and delivered through hidden runtime context. The trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.


**Live Telegram/forum transport proof (redacted):** A real Telegram forum reply was sent in an existing group topic on 2026-06-05 UTC. No live OpenClaw install or runtime was changed for this transport proof.

Redacted inbound metadata supplied to the agent turn:

```json
{
  "channel": "telegram",
  "surface": "telegram",
  "chat_type": "group",
  "is_forum": true,
  "topic_id": "<redacted-topic>",
  "message_id": "<redacted-current>",
  "reply_to_id": "<redacted-anchor>",
  "was_mentioned": true,
  "has_reply_context": true
}
```

The assembled current message included a Telegram reply target for the proof anchor while the persisted session user message remained the user-authored text only:

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "sourceChannel": "telegram",
  "content": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745"
}
```

Then the PR branch was exercised with the same redacted Telegram/forum shape through the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper:

```json
{
  "prompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "modelPrompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsForumSignal": true
}
```

This pairs a real Telegram/forum reply event with the PR-branch runtime handoff: the transport supplies `reply_to_id`/forum/mention facts, the session prompt remains clean user text, and the PR branch keeps reply/forum metadata in hidden runtime context instead of visible/model prompt text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against this PR branch, because Sean's live OpenClaw runtime was intentionally not changed. The local checkout does not have `rastermill` hydrated, so the broader embedded-attempt context-engine shard currently fails during media imports before reaching these assertions; I did not run dependency install as part of this PR polish.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 36 tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- attempted `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - reply/runtime shards passed, embedded-attempt shard was blocked by missing local package `rastermill`

target.title: Add diarized JSON transcript segments to media-understanding audio providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds structured diarized transcript segment support to media-understanding audio provider results.
- Lets OpenAI-compatible audio transcription providers receive optional `response_format` and `chunking_strategy` multipart fields via existing `tools.media.audio.providerOptions.<provider>` configuration.
- Preserves provider-returned `segments[]` on `AudioTranscriptionResult` / `MediaUnderstandingOutput` while keeping the existing transcript-only `text` behavior unchanged.
- Re-exports `AudioTranscriptSegment` from the media-understanding plugin SDK surface for provider authors.

## Why

Some OpenAI-compatible transcription APIs can return richer diarized JSON responses when requested with fields such as:

```json
{
  "response_format": "diarized_json",
  "chunking_strategy": "auto"
}
```

Before this change, the media-understanding audio provider seam allowed provider-specific options, but the OpenAI-compatible audio helper only sent `model`, `language`, and `prompt` as multipart fields. Provider-returned diarized `segments[]` also had no typed path through the audio result/output contracts, so speaker/timestamp metadata could not be preserved for downstream consumers.

This PR keeps the default transcript-only path intact while allowing configured providers to opt into JSON diarization. It only preserves structured segment data; it does not add transcript rendering, UI, or export behavior.

## Scope

Changed files:

- `src/media-understanding/types.ts`
- `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts`
- `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.ts`
- `src/plugin-sdk/media-understanding.ts`
- `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`
- `src/media-understanding/runner.deepgram.test.ts`

Not changed:

- No default audio provider selection changes.
- No channel-specific behavior changes.
- No rendering/export changes for `segments[]`.
- No live/service configuration changes.

## Implementation notes

- `AudioTranscriptionRequest` now has optional `responseFormat` and `chunkingStrategy` fields.
- `AudioTranscriptionResult` and `MediaUnderstandingOutput` now have optional `segments?: AudioTranscriptSegment[]`.
- Existing `providerOptions.<provider>.response_format` / `chunking_strategy` values are mapped into the new request fields for audio providers.
- OpenAI-compatible audio requests append `response_format` and `chunking_strategy` to the multipart form when provided.
- Non-JSON `response_format` values are rejected in this media-understanding helper because this path parses JSON responses.
- `segments` is only included when provider output actually contains valid segment text, avoiding an own `segments: undefined` property on normal transcript-only results.

## Validation

- `git diff HEAD^ --check`
- `node --experimental-strip-types --check` on modified source/test files
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`
  - 7 tests passed
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts src/media-understanding/runner.deepgram.test.ts`
  - 2 tests passed
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm run build:strict-smoke`
- Follow-up rebase onto current `main` to pick up upstream CI fixes; targeted wizard regression `src/wizard/setup.official-plugins.test.ts` passed (6 tests).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription providers can request diarized JSON (`response_format=diarized_json`, `chunking_strategy=auto`), and OpenClaw preserves provider-returned `segments[]` with speaker labels and timestamps through the media-understanding audio result/output types.
- **Real environment tested**: Non-production OCI GenAI OpenAI-compatible audio transcription setup using synthetic WAV fixtures; disposable OpenClaw source checkout after applying this PR. No production/live OpenClaw Gateway, runtime state, or config was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Ran a real provider smoke against the OCI GenAI OpenAI-compatible audio transcription endpoint with `model=openai.gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, `response_format=diarized_json`, and `chunking_strategy=auto` on synthetic multi-speaker WAV fixtures.
  2. In the disposable OpenClaw source checkout for this PR, ran targeted media-understanding seam checks and build/type checks after rebasing onto current `main`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Sanitized terminal/output excerpts from the real provider smoke and after-patch source seam validation:

  ```text
  real provider smoke (synthetic multi-speaker WAV)
  provider=oci-genai
  provider_api=openai-compatible-audio-transcriptions
  model=openai.gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize
  request fields: response_format=diarized_json, chunking_strategy=auto
  http.status=200, content_type=application/json
  output: segments=2, provider speakers=A/B
  segment[0]: speaker=A, start=0.00s, end=5.75s, text="The first speaker says the OCI media path should remain a fallback until quality checks pass."
  segment[1]: speaker=B, start=6.65s, end=11.80s, text="The second speaker asks for latency and reliability checks before activation."

  real provider smoke (longer mixed-language WAV)
  http.status=200, content_type=application/json
  output: segments=7, provider speakers=A/B, warnings=[]
  representative segment: speaker=B, start=8.30s, end=12.80s, text="今天我们测试英文和中文混合的语音识别。"

  after-patch disposable source validation
  src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts: 7 tests passed
  src/media-understanding/runner.deepgram.test.ts: 2 tests passed
  src/wizard/setup.official-plugins.test.ts: 6 tests passed
  pnpm run tsgo:test:src: passed
  pnpm run build:strict-smoke: passed
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real provider returned diarized JSON `segments[]` with speaker, start/end timestamp, and text fields. The patched OpenClaw audio helper/runner contracts preserve valid segment arrays on `AudioTranscriptionResult` / `MediaUnderstandingOutput` while leaving the existing transcript-only `text` path unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: This was not run against a production/live Gateway instance, and this PR does not add UI rendering or export formatting for `segments[]`.

target.title: /openclaw/src/chat/canvas-render.ts 添加注释
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Why does this matter now?

What is the intended outcome?

What is intentionally out of scope?

What does success look like?

What should reviewers focus on?

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
- Real environment tested:
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
- Observed result after fix:
- What was not tested:
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

What regression coverage was added or updated?

What failed before this fix, if known?

If no test was added, why not?

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

How is that risk mitigated?

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>

target.title: ci: gate stable releases on Windows companion assets
target.body:
## Summary

- require stable release publish to receive an explicit `windows_node_tag` and candidate-approved `windows_node_installer_digests` map before any publish child runs
- compare the live source release installer digests with the approved map, carry the pinned map unchanged through Windows promotion, and verify exact target companion assets plus exact checksum-manifest membership immediately before publishing the GitHub release draft
- make direct Windows promotion/recovery reject stale or extra `OpenClawCompanion-*` target assets, while keeping the installer lists as the explicit future-MSIX extension point
- update the release-candidate helper, release docs, and maintainer skill so canonical stable invocation sequences pass both the tag and approved digest map

## Verification

- `pnpm test test/scripts/release-candidate-checklist.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
  - passed: 2 files, 53 tests
- `pnpm check:workflows`
  - passed: zizmor and workflow interpolation checks
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
  - passed: 677 files
- `pnpm format:check -- .agents/skills/release-openclaw-maintainer/SKILL.md .github/workflows/openclaw-release-publish.yml .github/workflows/windows-node-release.yml docs/ci.md docs/reference/RELEASING.md scripts/release-candidate-checklist.mjs test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/release-candidate-checklist.test.ts`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed
- `node -e "const fs=require('fs');const yaml=require('yaml');for (const f of ['.github/workflows/openclaw-release-publish.yml','.github/workflows/windows-node-release.yml']) { yaml.parse(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('parsed '+f); }"`
  - parsed both changed workflows
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell --timing-json -- "pnpm check:changed"`
  - passed: provider `blacksmith-testbox`, Testbox `tbx_01ktz44b6neac09c06v942c7tm`, GitHub Actions run `27449837177`, exit 0
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --type Standard_D4ads_v6 --shell --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`
  - passed: provider `azure`, lease `cbx_67aa7dcc4b29`, run `run_28fa51dde647`, exit 0
- native Windows PowerShell recovery-guard probe through Crabbox
  - passed: provider `azure`, lease `cbx_6601e641f33b`, run `run_a81860329ed2`; exact stale-target filtering and stable/correction tag classification
- direct jq checksum-manifest parser probes
  - passed: the exact two-entry manifest parses; malformed and extra-blank-line manifests exit nonzero instead of silently dropping lines
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=kepler-alpha --thinking codex=high --stream-engine-output`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch correct, confidence 0.90

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Stable OpenClaw GitHub publication can no longer proceed without an explicitly approved Windows-node installer identity, successful signed-asset promotion, and an exact current companion asset/checksum contract.

Real environment tested: GitHub release APIs/download endpoints, Blacksmith Testbox, and an AWS native Windows Crabbox lease.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Read-only validation of `openclaw/openclaw-windows-node@v0.6.3`; latest OpenClaw release asset/download inspection; native Windows download, SHA-256, and `Get-AuthenticodeSignature` probe for both v0.6.3 installers; direct jq malformed-manifest probes; native Windows PowerShell recovery-guard probe; final current-head `pnpm check:changed` on Azure Crabbox.

Evidence after fix:

- current stable `openclaw/openclaw@v2026.6.6` contains only `openclaw-2026.6.6-dependency-evidence.zip`; the x64 installer, arm64 installer, and checksum latest-download URLs each returned HTTP 404
- source `openclaw/openclaw-windows-node@v0.6.3` is published/non-prerelease with:
  - `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-x64.exe`: `sha256:595a78a223f771adbaead34d56b190f5be5f97222d7707f8013ff4cc81d26983`
  - `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-arm64.exe`: `sha256:cb33a5f802f5790b2ac25bcb12cc3c6cc98ef5fa9fa8af5f0133f2b47cfcb88a`
- AWS Windows Crabbox provider/lease/run: `aws` / `cbx_07d0e0962266` / `run_a3307ad2fca3`
- both installers matched the pinned hashes and returned Authenticode status `Valid`
- exact signer subject for both: `CN=OpenClaw Foundation, O=OpenClaw Foundation, L=Mill Valley, S=California, C=US`
- signer thumbprint for both: `27878F3064C86E08F9D1AB3885D34C0821887A80`

Observed result after fix: The stable flow has a continuous approved-identity chain from release-candidate evidence through parent prevalidation, child hash/signature verification, exact recovery inventory, and the final pre-undraft asset/manifest gate. Malformed manifest lines fail closed, and direct recovery rejects unexpected target companion assets before upload while deriving stable/correction policy from the target tag.

What was not tested: The mutating Windows promotion workflow and release publication were intentionally not dispatched.

## Maintenance refresh (2026-06-13)

- rebased onto `openclaw/openclaw@7bd533a80e9d622966cd31b9a80ec284f2fc9dd9`; resolved the release-publish workflow conflict while preserving the upstream split ClawHub publish path

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extension’s `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: feat(hooks): per-turn usageState on reply_payload_sending
target.body:
## What

Attaches a per-turn execution snapshot to the `reply_payload_sending` plugin hook as an optional `usageState` field, so hook consumers can render per-response model/usage/context metadata without extra side calls.

`agent-runner` records the snapshot after the model turn. `dispatch` attaches it to the live `reply_payload_sending` event only when it can correlate the payload to the exact agent run id.

## Contract

`usageState` is best-effort live turn metadata. It is omitted for durable/recovered replay and any delivery path without exact run correlation.

Fields are optional/additive. Existing hook consumers are unaffected.

Per-turn atoms included:
- model/routing: provider, model, resolved/requested refs, reasoning effort, fast mode, fallback, auth mode, override source
- usage/context: token usage, last-call usage, cache usage, context budget, compaction count
- cost/timing: estimated turn cost, duration
- identity: agent id/session id/chat type plus configured agent identity

Provider subscription/limit windows are intentionally not included. The current run path does not carry exact credential identity for every provider/account, so attaching quota windows here would risk showing another profile's limits.

## Review response

Maintainer repair on 2026-06-13:
- Removed the provider-limit helper and `usageState.limits` contract from this PR.
- Changed usageState handoff to exact run-id correlation only; no session-key fallback.
- Shared the generated run id from `onAgentRunStart` into the delivery hook, so normal live replies still get the snapshot even when the caller did not provide `replyOptions.runId`.
- Documented the live/best-effort hook behavior in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`.
- Trimmed excessive comments and removed stale limit tests/code.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-usage-state.test.ts src/plugins/wired-hooks-reply-payload-sending.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt 'Review PR 89629 after maintainer repair. Focus on exact run correlation for reply usageState, provider limit scoping, public hook docs, and regressions introduced by the repair commit.'`


## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: This PR exposes live per-turn `usageState` on `reply_payload_sending` so hook consumers can render model, usage, context, cost, timing, routing, and identity atoms without side calls. Provider subscription/limit windows are intentionally not part of this PR after maintainer repair because the live run path does not carry exact credential identity for every provider/account.

Real environment tested: The contributor tested the `usageState` contract on a live OpenClaw gateway with downstream footer consumption in Telegram and Discord. The PR itself has no in-core UX; the proof uses a hook consumer to show the live contract data reaching real channel replies.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and deployed the branch to a live OpenClaw gateway, sent real replies through Telegram and Discord, and observed a downstream `reply_payload_sending` footer consumer render the live `usageState` atoms.

Evidence after fix: Live downstream screenshots from the contributor show the footer rendered from `usageState` data: Telegram https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-proof/proof_telegram2.png and Discord https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-proof/proof_discord1.jpeg

Observed result after fix: Real Telegram and Discord replies included the footer generated from the hook `usageState` data. After maintainer repair, the supported contract is the same live per-turn `usageState` delivery minus provider limit windows; uncorrelated and durable replay paths intentionally omit it.

What was not tested: No fresh maintainer-run live gateway screenshot was captured after removing the provider-limit field. The live proof covers downstream `usageState` delivery; scoped maintainer tests cover the repaired exact-run correlation and omission behavior.
